items.title: US, Mexico Agree On 1-Month Tariff Pause: Stocks Rebound, Peso Rallies
items.author: Piero Cingari
items.description: The U.S. and Mexico have agreed to pause planned trade tariffs for one month, signaling a shift in the trade dispute. The agreement includes Mexico's commitment to border security and upcoming high-level trade negotiations.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The news of the tariff pause and upcoming trade talks between the U.S. and Mexico is likely to have a positive impact on the Mexican economy and the iShares MSCI Mexico ETF, which tracks the performance of Mexican stocks.

The S&P 500, as tracked by the SPDR S&P 500 ETF, trimmed its losses and rebounded as traders anticipated a potential de-escalation in trade tensions between the U.S. and Mexico, which is seen as a positive development for the broader U.S. stock market.

items.title: US Manufacturing Confidence Tops Forecasts, Yet Price Pressures Intensify, 'Could Become A Concern,' Economist Says
items.author: Piero Cingari
items.description: U.S. manufacturing unexpectedly expanded in January, but rising input costs pose a growing inflation risk. Markets reacted negatively to new tariffs, with the S&P 500 falling and Treasury yields dropping.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The S&P 500 fell 1.2% in reaction to the announcement of new tariffs.
The yield on the 30-year Treasury bond dropped 5 basis points, pushing the iShares 20+ Year Treasury Bond ETF up 0.7%, signaling demand for safe-haven assets.
